vulnerability.title: Add nodev Option to /dev/shm
vulnerability.description: The nodev mount option can be used to prevent creation of device files in /dev/shm. Legitimate character and block devices should not exist within temporary directories like /dev/shm. Add the nodev option to the fourth column of /etc/fstab for the line which controls mounting of /dev/shm.
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if ( ! ( { rpm --quiet -q kernel-core ;} && { rpm --quiet -q rpm-ostree ;} && { rpm --quiet -q bootc ;} && { ! rpm --quiet -q openshift-kubelet ;} && ([ -f /run/ostree-booted ] || [ -L /ostree ]) ) && ! ( [ -f /.dockerenv ] || [ -f /run/.containerenv ] ) ); then function perform_remediation { mount_point_match_regexp="$(printf "^[[:space:]]*[^#].*[[:space:]]%s[[:space:]]" /dev/shm)" # If the mount point is not in /etc/fstab, get previous mount options from /etc/mtab if ! grep -q "$mount_point_match_regexp" /etc/fstab; then # runtime opts without some automatic kernel/userspace-added defaults previous_mount_opts=$(grep "$mount_point_match_regexp" /etc/mtab | head -1 | awk '{print $4}' \ | sed -E "s/(rw|defaults|seclabel|nodev)(,|$)//g;s/

triage_decision.reason: The fix adds the nodev mount option to /dev/shm in /etc/fstab to prevent creation of device files in temporary directories. This is a preventive security measure that is unlikely to cause system lockout or breakage, so we prefer safe_to_remediate per policy.

previous_attempts.scan_output: Single-rule scan failed for mount_option_dev_shm_nodev (rule=xccdf_org.ssgproject.content_rule_mount_option_dev_shm_nodev). Could not verify.

llm_verdict.message:
We are to write a tool named `remediate_finding_mount_option_dev_shm_nodev` that remediates the finding: "Add nodev option to /dev/shm".

The tool will:
  1. Read the file `/etc/fstab`.
  2. For each non-empty, non-comment line in the file:
        - If the line has at least two fields (after splitting by whitespace and discarding empty fields) and the first field is exactly `/dev/shm` and the second field is exactly `/dev/shm`, then:
            - If the line has at least 4 fields, replace the fourth field (index 3) with the current fourth field appended by ",nodev".
            - Else if the line has exactly 3 fields, set the fourth field to ",nodev".
            - Otherwise, leave the line unchanged.
        - Otherwise, leave the line unchanged.
  3. Write the modified content back to `/etc/fstab`.

We assume that the file `/etc/fstab` uses whitespace as the delimiter and that comments (lines starting with `#`) are to be skipped.

We'll use the following functions from the provided tools:
  - `read_file` to read `/etc/fstab`.
  - `write_file` to write the modified content back to `/etc/fstab`.

We do not use `run_cmd` because we are not executing any shell commands.

Let's write the code accordingly.
